assertions.assertion: Defines a simulator that generates AR(2) time series of length 100
assertions.evidence: Lines 29-64: T=100 is set, observation_model generates AR(2) series with y[t] = phi1*y[t-1] + phi2*y[t-2] + noise. Line 67: bf.make_simulator([prior, observation_model]).

assertions.assertion: Routes the time series through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
assertions.evidence: Line 127: .concatenate(["y"], into="summary_variables") — y is routed into summary_variables, not inference_conditions.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT use SetTransformer for the time series data
assertions.evidence: Line 94: Uses bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer, not SetTransformer. No SetTransformer appears anywhere.

assertions.assertion: Uses TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or FusionTransformer as the summary network
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) for the noise standard deviation
Does NOT flatten the time series into a single vector for inference_conditions
assertions.evidence: Line 123: .as_time_series(["y"]) reshapes to (T,1). Line 127: y goes into summary_variables. No flattening.

assertions.assertion: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
assertions.evidence: Uses ad hoc SetTransformer sizes (embed_dims=(64,64), num_heads=(4,4), mlp_widths=(128,128)) with no reference to model-sizes.md. Analysis notes describe it as 'default-sized architecture'.

assertions.assertion: Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Saves and inspects training history (JSON + inspect_training or equivalent)
assertions.evidence: Training history inspected via printed summary and matplotlib plot but NOT saved as JSON. No call to inspect_training or equivalent. History only saved as a PNG plot.

assertions.assertion: Runs in-silico diagnostics and checks against house thresholds
assertions.evidence: Lines 192-209: compute_default_diagnostics called but no threshold checks applied. Metrics printed and saved to CSV but never compared against pass/fail criteria. Uses fabricated bf.diagnostics.run_sbc() with non-standard thresholds.

assertions.assertion: Accesses posterior samples by original names (phi1, phi2, sigma), NOT 'inference_variables'
assertions.evidence: Lines 224-227: samples["phi1"], samples["phi2"], samples["sigma"] accessed by original parameter names.
